description: Add user authentication with login and registration functionality. Users will be able to create accounts, log in, and access protected features. Posts will be associated with authors.

tasks.task:
Create the Auth module with user authentication:

1. Create a new `auth/` directory with the NestJS module pattern:
   - `auth.module.ts` - Auth module definition
   - `auth.controller.ts` - Endpoints: POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, GET /auth/me
   - `auth.service.ts` - Business logic for user management and authentication

2. Create a `users/` directory for user data:
   - `users.service.ts` - In-memory user storage (following the pattern in `blog.service.ts`)
   - Define User interface: { id: number, email: string, password: string, name: string, createdAt: Date }

3. Implement endpoints:
   - POST /auth/register - Accept { email, name, password }, validate email uniqueness, store user, return user (without password)
   - POST /auth/login - Accept { email, password }, validate credentials, return { user, token } (simple token for now)
   - GET /auth/me - Verify token from Authorization header, return current user

4. Add simple JWT-like token generation (base64 encoded user ID for simplicity, or install @nestjs/jwt)

5. Register AuthModule in app.module.ts (following the BlogModule pattern)

6. Enable CORS for frontend access (check main.ts)

Create authentication UI components and state management:

1. Create new components in `src/components/`:
   - `LoginForm.tsx` - Email/password form following CreatePost.tsx pattern with useState for form fields, submitting state, and error handling
   - `RegisterForm.tsx` - Name/email/password form with same patterns
   - `AuthHeader.tsx` - Shows logged-in user name or Login/Register buttons

2. Add authentication state to App.tsx:
   - Add state: `const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)`
   - Add state: `const [showLogin, setShowLogin] = useState(false)`
   - Add state: `const [showRegister, setShowRegister] = useState(false)`
   - Store token in localStorage on successful login/register
   - On app load, check localStorage for token and validate with GET /auth/me

3. Update App.tsx to show auth UI:
   - Add AuthHeader at top of the app
   - Conditionally render LoginForm or RegisterForm based on state

4. Add CSS styles to `style.css`:
   - `.auth-header` - Header bar with user info/buttons
   - `.login-form`, `.register-form` - Following `.create-post-form` pattern

5. Define User interface in a types file or in App.tsx:
   - `interface User { id: number, email: string, name: string }`

Associate posts with authors:

1. Update the Post interface in `blog.service.ts`:
   - Add `authorId: number` and `authorName: string` fields

2. Update `blog.controller.ts`:
   - Modify POST /posts to optionally accept authorId from request body or extract from auth header
   - Update the create method to include author information

3. Update existing seed posts in `blog.service.ts` to have author information (can use a default 'Anonymous' or system user)

Update post creation to include author:

1. Update `CreatePost.tsx`:
   - Accept user prop: `interface CreatePostProps { ... user?: User }`
   - Include authorId in the POST request body when user is logged in

2. Update `PostList.tsx` and `PostDetail.tsx`:
   - Display author name on posts
   - Update Post interface to include `authorId?: number` and `authorName?: string`

3. Update App.tsx:
   - Pass user to CreatePost component

example_backend: POST /auth/register with valid data returns new user without password
POST /auth/register with duplicate email returns error
POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns user and token
POST /auth/login with invalid credentials returns 401 error
GET /auth/me with valid token returns current user
POST /posts with author information creates post with author
example_frontend: Register form submits and creates new account
Register form shows validation errors for duplicate email
Login form authenticates and stores token
Login form shows error for invalid credentials
Auth header shows user name when logged in
Auth header shows Login/Register buttons when logged out
Logout clears token and shows login buttons
Creating a post includes author name when logged in
Posts display author name in list and detail views
